name: Chocolate-Chocolate-Chocolate-Chip Fudge Cake
recipeIngredient: 3/4 Cup Unsweetened cocoa powder (preferably Dutch process)
3 1/2 Teaspoons Unsweetened cocoa powder (preferably Dutch process)
2 Tablespoons Unsweetened cocoa powder (preferably Dutch process)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the cake layers: Preheat oven to 350°F. Line two 9-inch round cake pans with waxed paper, then butter and flour the pans, tapping out any excess flour.
2. In a mixer bowl, beat butter until light and fluffy. Gradually add sugar, beating until smooth. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
3. Sift together flour, cocoa, baking soda, baking powder, and salt. Alternately add milk with beaten eggs, mixing only until blended. Stir in vanilla and liqueur.
4. Divide batter between prepared pans. Bake 40-45 minutes or until tops are springy to the touch. Remove from oven and set on racks to cool for 30 minutes. Loosen edges with a knife, unmold cakes, peel off waxed paper, and let cool completely.
5. Prepare the fudge filling: Combine sugar, cream, chocolate, and corn syrup in a small heavy saucepan. Simmer over moderate heat, stirring frequently until mixture thickens (about 10 minutes). Remove from heat, dot with butter, and let cool to room temperature.
6. Prepare the chocolate cream: Beat cream and cocoa until soft peaks form. Gradually add confectioners' sugar, beating until stiff.
7. Assemble the cake: Spread fudge filling over one cake layer, then sprinkle evenly with chocolate chips. Spread half of chocolate cream on top of chips, then place second cake layer on top. Cover sides and top with remaining chocolate cream. Decorate as desired using pastry bag.
8. Prepare the syrup: Combine cocoa, corn syrup, sugar, and water in a small heavy saucepan. Simmer over low heat for 2 minutes, stirring constantly. Transfer to bowl and let cool to room temperature.
9. Just before serving, drizzle syrup over cake in a lacy design. Refrigerate up to 3 hours before serving.
